gaps: DoorDash does not provide a stable public DoorDash API for this surface.
Direct endpoint mirrors are risky because cart and order mutations are interleaved with read operations.
Agent-facing use needs redacted, read-focused commands and explicit guardrails around cart and checkout actions.
patterns: Use browser-session cookies and CSRF headers for DoorDash web GraphQL compatibility.
Expose safe read commands for store search, menus, item options, order history, addresses, and payment summaries.
Keep cart and order placement behind explicit flags and confirmation-oriented command boundaries.

novel_features.name: Safe store search
novel_features.description: Search DoorDash stores through the consumer GraphQL autocomplete feed without mutating cart or order state.
novel_features.rationale: Agents need store discovery that avoids cart side effects and hides raw GraphQL plumbing.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Useful for meal/vendor research while preserving account safety.

novel_features.name: Menu and option inspection
novel_features.description: Fetch DoorDash store menus in a normalized shape without changing the cart.
novel_features.rationale: The raw storepageFeed response is large and noisy; the command reduces it to agent-usable menu data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents compare menu choices before any cart mutation is considered.

novel_features.name: Item customization discovery
novel_features.description: Inspect item option groups and nested add-ons before adding anything to a cart.
novel_features.rationale: DoorDash item customization is deeply nested; exposing it separately reduces accidental invalid cart mutations.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents can reason about required modifiers before proposing a cart change.

novel_features.name: Redacted account reads
novel_features.description: Read recent DoorDash order summaries without placing a new order.
novel_features.rationale: Historical order lookup is useful context, but public release artifacts must avoid leaking sensitive details.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Supports repeat-order and preference analysis without checkout risk.

novel_features.name: Guarded cart commands
novel_features.description: Expose cart inspection and mutations as a separately named, guarded command family rather than mixing them into search/menu reads.
novel_features.rationale: Cart mutation is materially different from read-only browsing and must stay clearly separated for agents and reviewers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Clear command boundaries reduce accidental purchase-flow side effects.

narrative.headline: DoorDash GraphQL tooling with safe read commands and guarded cart/order boundaries.
narrative.value_prop: This CLI packages a curated DoorDash GraphQL surface for agents. It separates safe browsing and account reads from cart/order mutations so reviewers can evaluate provenance, safety, and shipped command behavior without confusing the generated Go artifact with the active Node/CycleTLS runtime.
narrative.auth_narrative: DoorDash access uses a browser-session cookie plus CSRF header. Do not publish real cookies, CSRF values, addresses, payment IDs, or order details; configure credentials only in the local runtime environment.

quickstart.comment: Check local config and generated CLI health.
Run a safe read-only store search.
Read a small account-history sample without checkout.

troubleshoots.fix: Refresh the local browser-session cookie and CSRF header; never commit those values.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this skill for the generated Go DoorDash PP artifact and its formal provenance/release checks. For day-to-day live DoorDash use in Mike's Hermes environment, prefer the active Node/CycleTLS wrapper documented separately in docs/SKILL.md.

recipes.title: Search stores safely
recipes.explanation: Uses the safe curated search command rather than a raw GraphQL mutation.

recipes.title: Inspect generated cart boundary
recipes.explanation: Shows the guarded mutation family without mutating cart state.
